_**Agent (default)**_

Root cause looks like a bootstrap auth loop: `config-provider` loads config before any token exists, but `configList` is generated as `secure`, so the backend can return an HTML auth/index page instead of JSON, causing the `Unexpected token '<'` parse error. I’m going to make config loading explicitly non-secure and add a clearer message when HTML is returned.

_**Agent (default)**_

_**Agent (default)**_

You’re correct — the crash is happening in `src/providers/config-provider.tsx`, and I fixed it there.

- `client.api.configList()` was being called as a secure request during bootstrap (before auth is available).
- In that state, the server can return HTML (e.g. auth/index page), which caused JSON parsing to fail with `Unexpected token '<'`.
- I updated both config fetch calls to explicitly use `client.api.configList({ secure: false })` in `loadConfig` and `reloadConfig`.
- I also added a targeted error message for HTML responses to make this failure mode obvious.

Changed file:
- `src/providers/config-provider.tsx`

Lint check:
- No linter issues in that file.

If you refresh now and still see the same failure, that means `/api/config` itself is being routed to HTML server-side; then we should fix backend/proxy routing/auth rules for that endpoint.

_**Agent (default)**_

The new message exposed the underlying config mismatch, and I’ve fixed it in code.

What was happening:
- Your setup/docs used `CORE_API`, but runtime URL building only read `VITE_API_URL`.
- When `VITE_API_URL` was empty, requests went to frontend origin (`:8081`), so `/api/config` returned HTML instead of JSON.

What I changed:
- `src/lib/network/utils.ts`
  - `buildAbsoluteUrl()` now supports both:
    - `VITE_API_URL` (preferred)
    - `CORE_API` (legacy fallback)
- `src/vite-env.d.ts`
  - Added typing for `CORE_API`.
- `src/providers/config-provider.tsx` (previous fix still applied)
  - `configList()` is called with `{ secure: false }` during bootstrap.
- Docs updated for clarity:
  - `.env.sample`
  - `README.md` (uses `VITE_API_URL`, keeps `CORE_API` as legacy, corrected dev port to `8081`)

What you should do now:
1. Ensure `.env` contains either:
   - `VITE_API_URL=http://<your-backend-host:port>`
   - or `CORE_API=http://<your-backend-host:port>`
2. Restart the Vite dev server (`npm start`) so env changes are picked up.
3. Refresh the app.

If it still fails, I can add a quick runtime debug log to print the resolved API base URL and verify exactly where `/api/config` is being sent.

---

_**User**_

I found issue when I navigate though the apps I have full refresh of the page

I’ll track down where navigation is using browser-level redirects (`href`/`window.location`) instead of React Router navigation, then patch those spots so moving through modules stays client-side without full reloads.

_**Agent (default)**_

I found a routing smell that can cause unnecessary remount-like behavior on module switches: child routes are defined as absolute paths inside a nested route tree. I’m going to normalize them to `index` + relative `:moduleName/*` so navigation stays fully in-router.

_**Agent (default)**_

Good catch — I fixed one routing setup that can feel like full page refresh during module navigation.

What I changed:
- In `src/app.tsx`, normalized nested routes from absolute child paths to proper nested config:
  - `path={rootRoute}` child -> `index`
  - `path="/:moduleName/*"` -> `path=":moduleName/*"`

Why this helps:
- Absolute child paths inside a nested route tree can cause avoidable remount behavior.
- Using `index` + relative child paths keeps transitions in the same router branch and avoids refresh-like reinitialization.

Please test by clicking through sidebar modules again.  
If you still see hard refreshes, I’ll do a second pass focused on auth redirects (`window.location.replace`) and move them to router navigation flow.

---

_**User**_

okay now all works good
I want you to add loading state while new page is loading, to have better ui and ux

---

_**Agent (default)**_

Great — I’ll add a polished route-loading UI so module transitions show a clear loading state instead of abrupt content switches. I’ll implement it in the shared module loader so it applies across the app.

_**Agent (default)**_

Much better now — I replaced the heavy full-page fallback with a **non-flashy transition loader**.

What changed in `src/features/module-loader/index.tsx`:
- Added a **180ms delay** before showing loader (prevents flash on quick navigations).
- Replaced skeleton card screen with:
  - a subtle top `LinearProgress`
  - small `Loading...` label
  - gentle `Fade` in animation

Why this feels better:
- Fast route changes: no loader appears, so no flicker.
- Slower changes: users still get clear feedback, but without abrupt screen replacement.

If you want it even smoother, I can tune the delay (`120ms` / `250ms`) to match your preference.
